messages.content:
checking this paragraph "
This email is regarding a reference check conducted for the candidate, U Chan Nyein who has applied for "Manager" position at the General Administration Department in our organization. You have been listed as a point of contact for reference checks regarding his past work experience and performance.

I will be attaching reference check form that we usually send out to references to have a deeper insight into candidates. I would be very glad if you filled it out and sent back the completed form during the next week.

Thank you so much for your time and feel free to reach out to me if you have any questions.

As an AI language model, I'm not sure what you want me to check, but I can suggest proofreading edits to this paragraph:

This email is regarding a reference check for the "Manager" position at the General Administration Department of our organization, conducted for the candidate, U Chan Nyein. You have been listed as a point of contact for reference checks concerning his past work performance and experience.

I will be attaching the reference check form that we usually send out to request references for a deeper insight into candidates. I would be very grateful if you completed and returned the form during the next week.

Thank you for your time, and please don't hesitate to reach out to me if you have any questions.
is it correct " I am going for interview with admin team and let me skip this meeting"
The sentence "I am going for an interview with the admin team, so let me skip this meeting" would be more grammatically correct and clear in its meaning.
how to reply email if you couldn't join the interview due to other appointment as schedule

Here is an example of an email you could send if you couldn't join an interview due to another appointment as scheduled:

Dear [Interviewer Name],

Thank you very much for considering my application for the [position name] role. I regret to inform you that, due to a prior commitment that cannot be changed, I will not be able to make it to the interview for the time slot scheduled on [date and time].

I understand that my absence will cause inconvenience, and I apologise for any trouble or inconvenience caused. I value the opportunity to be part of [company name] and would appreciate another chance to meet and discuss the role further at your convenience.

Please accept my apologies and let me know if it is possible to arrange another interview time at your earliest convenience.

Thank you,

[Your name]

how to reply this email "Thank you for your time and effort to be part of Talent Acquisition Journey at Yoma Bank.

 

It was our pleasure to learn about you, especially your skills and accomplishments.

 

We met many candidates who are all as strong as you are, and had to make a challenging decision based on the perspectives of the best team/culture & organization fit.

 

Unfortunately, our current requirements do not match with your profile and hence, we have decided not to proceed with your application of Talent Acquisition Manager position for this time round.

 

However, please allow us to keep your profile in our Talent Bank for the next 3 months should there be any future opportunities that would match with your profile.

 

Once again, thank you very much your time and your interest in Yoma Bank.

 

We encourage you to check our career announcement at JobNet for all recruitment updates.

 

We wish your health and success.

Dear [Hiring Manager Name],

Thank you for taking the time to consider me for the Talent Acquisition Manager position at Yoma Bank. Though I am disappointed that my application was not successful this time round, I appreciate your kind message and your offer to keep my profile in your Talent Bank for future opportunities that may arise.

I would like to thank you and your team for the opportunity, and I am grateful for the positive feedback on my skills and accomplishments. I understand that you had to make a tough decision to choose from many strong candidates, and I appreciate your professionalism and candor throughout the process.

I will definitely keep an eye on your career announcements at JobNet and continue to be interested in potential opportunities with Yoma Bank in the future. 

Thank you once again for your consideration and your kind words.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
is it correct " have you finish preparation of offer letter ?

The sentence "Have you finished preparing the offer letter?" would be more grammatically correct. Here, the word "finish" should be changed to "finished" and "preparation" to "preparing" because the correct tense of the verb "finish" to use with the auxiliary verb "have" is "finished," which indicates past tense, and the correct form of the verb "prepare" to use in this sentence is "preparing," which indicates present continuous tense.
is it correct "If you don’t understand start from beginning, how can you manage these change management ?

The sentence "If you don’t understand from the start, how can you manage these changes in management?" would be more grammatically correct. In this sentence, "start from beginning" should be changed to "from the start" and "change management" should be changed to "changes in management" to make the meaning clear.

is it correct "
I have scheduled interviews with the candidates on June 27th (Tuesday) at 2:00 p.m. The purpose of the interviews is to discuss career in relation to the Section Head (Marketing & Merchant Management) role. She has been interviewed by CEO yesterday and this session is to meet with you as the further process of this recruitment.

The paragraph is almost correct. Here is a suggestion for how to edit it:

"I have scheduled interviews with the candidates for June 27th (Tuesday) at 2:00 p.m. The purpose of the interviews is to discuss their career history and experience in relation to the Section Head (Marketing & Merchant Management) role. The candidate has already been interviewed by the CEO, and this session is part of the recruitment process. I look forward to meeting you and discussing your thoughts on this candidate."  

Note that I have made changes to the language to make it sound more clear and professional.
create email when send the employment agreement to candidate who we hired as chief business officer role

Here is an example email to send the employment agreement to a candidate who has been hired for the Chief Business Officer role:

Subject: Employment Agreement for Chief Business Officer Role

Dear [Candidate's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I am delighted to inform you that we have selected you for the position of Chief Business Officer at our organization, and we would like to offer you an employment agreement for your consideration.

Attached to this email, please find the employment agreement that outlines the terms and conditions of the role, compensation, benefits, and other important details. We would be very grateful if you could review this document carefully and let us know if you have any questions or concerns.

Once you have had some time to review the agreement and are comfortable with the terms, please sign the document and send it back to me. We will then consider the agreement final, and we can proceed with the next steps in the onboarding process.

Please let me know if you need any additional information, and I would be happy to assist you. We are eager to have you on board as part of our team and look forward to the contribution you will make in this role.

Once again, congratulations, and we look forward to working with you soon.

Best regards,

[Your name]

Casual leave is a type of leave that is granted to employees for short-term personal reasons, such as attending a family function, attending to an emergency obligation, or handling unforeseen circumstances. Casual leave is typically granted after an employee has accumulated certain tenure in the company or an organization, and it is often paid leave that can be used in increments. The number of casual leaves available to an employee may vary from one company to another, and some companies may also have policies that regulate the use of casual leaves.
